source_label: Wikipedia вАФ Voronoi diagram (named after Georgy Voronoy)
notes: Stress placement is genuinely contested. Named after Russian mathematician Georgy Voronoy (–Т–Њ—А–Њ–љ√≥–є). The most commonly documented mainstream English form puts primary stress on the FIRST syllable вАФ VOR-uh-noy, /ЋИv…ФЋРr…Щn…Ф…™/ (so howtopronounce.com and the dev-community Casual Effects computational-graphics guide). But it competes with a final-stress vor-uh-NOY (ending like 'annoy') and a middle-stress vor-OH-noy вАФ no single anglicization has won. The Russian-accurate rendering is closer to 'vah-rah-NOY', with the unstressed o's reduced toward 'uh/ah'. Pervasive in computational geometry, mesh generation, procedural texturing, and game dev, where 'how do you even say Voronoi?' (usually alongside Delaunay) is a recurring forum thread.
